verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a soluble protein lacking a transmembrane domain. It is localized intracellularly: in the acrosomal lumen of sperm (an internal compartment that loses IZUMO4 after the acrosome reaction) and in cytoplasm/nucleus of somatic tissues. No TM anchor, GPI anchor, or documented covalent/wash-resistant surface tethering to a TM partner. No documented cell-state-induced, tissue-restricted, lysosomal-exocytosis, dual-localization, or stable-surface-attachment pool provides outer-leaflet accessibility. Paralog IZUMO1 is the surface-exposed sperm-egg fusion partner; IZUMO4 plays no central role in fusion and is not surface-accessible.
